rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4834 as the trigger (matching yenuo26's comment) but both overclaim a resolved state — asserting the level=1 fix is 'already in main' and recommending closure — when the actual thread shows the issue still under investigation, with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to help trigger a full CI-cluster sleep-mode test run; neither answer mentions this real next step. Y is more thorough and better grounded, citing the companion generate()-partial-wake guard, named regression tests, and giving actionable runtime guidance for users beyond just the CI failure, giving it an edge on grounding and completeness. X is serviceable but thinner and includes irrelevant leftover 'skill candidates' boilerplate that doesn't belong in a maintainer answer.
